untranslatable_reason: Signal requires detection of 'fundamental catalyst event identified on calendar' and 'consensus bias toward outcome' — these are external event/sentiment inputs not available as technical indicators. Calendar-based event detection and sentiment analysis require external data sources beyond the allowed indicator set. The core mechanic (enter before news, exit on release) cannot be purely mechanized without external event timestamps and consensus data.
translation_notes: Used ADX > 25 as proxy for 'strong directional momentum' and pivot levels as approximate support/resistance for exits. Stop loss set to 2.0% (80 pips context). However, this translation significantly degrades the signal intent, which fundamentally depends on external event calendar and sentiment data not available in technical indicators. A proper implementation would require: (1) external calendar input for event dates, (2) sentiment/consensus data source, (3) time-based logic to enter N days before scheduled events.
